content:
# Petrophysical Analysis

**Summary**: The measurement of physical properties like porosity and saturation in core or sample material.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-alkhunaizi|Alkhunaizi, 2024]] used petrophysical analysis to measure laboratory water saturation (Sw) and porosity.

- [[2025-oyekan|Oyekan, 2025]] applied petrophysical-analysis to link rock physics models with seismic wave velocities.

- [[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025]] performed petrophysical analysis to assess the impact of CO2 on rock properties.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2025-oyekan|Oyekan, 2025]] applied petrophysical-analysis to link rock physics models with seismic wave velocities.

- [[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025]] performed petrophysical analysis to assess the impact of CO2 on rock properties.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Laboratory Sw values ranged from 0.05 to 0.11 with a mean of 0.07. ([[2024-alkhunaizi|Alkhunaizi, 2024, p. 50]])

- Rock physics models provide the necessary mathematical links between mineralogy, pore geometry, and elastic properties. ([[2025-oyekan|Oyekan, 2025, p. 31]])

- Quantified changes in porosity and permeability following CO2 treatment. ([[2025-alhubail|Alhubail, 2025, p. 73]])
